mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (measured by GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes) signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60-day horizon. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2017 real GDP growth at 1.4% annualized
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% and continues climbing, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and raises refinancing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices equity valuations downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% and increases by >0.25pp within 30 days
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is positive (opposite of predicted direction).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate decline below 4.5% combined with accelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS month-over-month change >150k) signals tight labor markets and wage pressure. This interaction indicates late-cycle conditions where inflation expectations rise and equity risk premiums expand, producing negative 20-day forward returns as the market prices in Fed tightening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS monthly change > 150k for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% and PAYEMS at 146,063k as of April 2017
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of UNRATE and PAYEMS on forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth acceleration signals strengthening manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization. Rising INDPRO over a 3-month window predicts positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days as it reflects robust business investment and operational efficiency gains.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the relationship reverses sign in supply-shock regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month growth rate combined with housing starts (HOUST) acceleration indicates strengthening consumer demand and housing market health. When both series show positive momentum simultaneously, this interaction signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% in same month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression on forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth rate above 2.5% combined with the federal funds rate below 1.0% creates a negative real rate environment. This regime indicates monetary accommodation despite rising prices, which supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive 5-day forward returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI growth) shows no predictive power for forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward S&P 500 returns as investors reprice equity risk premiums upward in response to labor market weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by >0.3pp over any 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and forward returns is positive or near-zero (< 0.02 in absolute value).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,100k units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as housing weakness is a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown and reduced corporate earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,100k units for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or housing market contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST levels and forward returns is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
